title: The Lost Dog
paragraphs: Tom was walking in the park. He saw a small dog sitting alone under a tree.
The dog looked sad and hungry. Tom gave it some bread from his bag.
He checked the dog's collar. There was a phone number on it.
Tom called the number. A happy woman answered and came to pick up her dog.
She thanked Tom many times. Tom felt very good about helping.

questions.q: Where did Tom find the dog?
What did Tom give the dog?
How did Tom contact the owner?
opts: He called the number on the collar

title: A Cup of Coffee
paragraphs: Lan goes to the same coffee shop every morning before work.
One day, the barista said: 'Your coffee is free today. The person before you paid for it.'
Lan was surprised and very happy. She decided to pay for the next person's coffee too.
This made three people smile that morning. A small act of kindness can go a long way.

opts: She paid for the next person

questions.q: What is the lesson of the story?

title: The Job Interview
paragraphs: Minh had a job interview at 9 AM. He woke up late and panicked.
He quickly got dressed and ran to the bus stop. The bus was already leaving!
Minh took a taxi instead. He arrived at the office just in time.
The interview went well. The manager said: 'We will call you next week.'
Minh learned an important lesson: always set two alarms!

questions.q: How did Minh get to the interview?

title: The New Neighbor
paragraphs: A new family moved in next door. They were from another country.
At first, nobody talked to them. They looked lonely.
One day, Mai brought them some homemade cake. They were so grateful.
The mother said: 'Thank you! We don't know anyone here yet.'
Soon, they became good friends. Mai learned that being kind opens doors.

questions.q: Where was the new family from?

title: Learning to Swim
paragraphs: Hoa was afraid of water. All her friends could swim, but she couldn't.
Her father said: 'I will teach you. Don't worry, I'll be right beside you.'
Every weekend, they went to the pool together. At first, Hoa held her father's hand tightly.
After three months, Hoa could swim by herself! She was so proud.
Now she loves swimming. She even joined the school swim team.

questions.q: How long did it take Hoa to learn?

title: The Surprise Birthday Party
paragraphs: It was Linh's birthday, but nobody mentioned it at school. She felt sad.
After school, her best friend asked her to come to her house for 'homework help.'
When Linh walked in, everyone shouted: 'Surprise! Happy Birthday!'
The room was full of balloons and her favorite chocolate cake.
Linh cried happy tears. It was the best birthday she ever had.

questions.q: Why was Linh sad at first?
What was the excuse to bring Linh?
What kind of cake was there?

title: A Rainy Day Adventure
paragraphs: It rained heavily all morning. The children couldn't go outside to play.
'I'm bored!' said Tuan. His mother smiled and brought out a big cardboard box.
'Let's build a spaceship!' she said. They spent two hours cutting and painting.
When they finished, the box looked like a real spaceship. They played astronauts all afternoon.
Tuan realized that rainy days can be the most creative days.

questions.q: Why couldn't the children play outside?

title: The Magic Library
paragraphs: There was a small library at the end of the street that nobody visited.
One day, curious Huong walked in. The old librarian gave her a book and said: 'This book will change your life.'
Huong read the book in one night. It was about a girl who traveled the world.
After reading it, Huong felt inspired. She started studying English harder.
Years later, Huong became a travel writer. She always remembered that little library.

opts: At the end of the street
A girl who traveled the world

title: First Day at Work
paragraphs: It was Duc's first day at his new job. He was nervous and arrived thirty minutes early.
His manager showed him around the office and introduced him to the team.
At lunch, a colleague invited him to eat together. 'Don't worry, everyone is friendly here,' she said.
By the end of the day, Duc had learned many new things and made a few friends.
He went home feeling confident. 'Tomorrow will be even better,' he thought.

questions.q: How did Duc feel on his first day?
How did Duc feel at the end of the day?

title: The Street Food Chef
paragraphs: Grandma Thu had a small pho stall on the corner. She woke up at 4 AM every day to prepare the broth.
Her secret was slow-cooking the beef bones for eight hours with special herbs.
People lined up every morning. Some customers had been coming for twenty years.
One day, a food blogger wrote about her pho. Suddenly, tourists from everywhere came to try it.
Grandma Thu smiled and said: 'Good food needs patience and love.'

questions.q: What time did Grandma Thu wake up?
How long did she cook the broth?
What brought tourists to her stall?

title: The Exam
paragraphs: The final exam was tomorrow. Khanh had not studied much during the semester.
He stayed up all night trying to memorize everything. By morning, he was exhausted.
During the exam, his mind went blank. He couldn't remember anything he had studied.
He barely passed with a very low score. He felt disappointed in himself.
From that day, Khanh promised to study a little every day, not just before exams.

title: The Lost Phone
paragraphs: Nga left her phone on the bus by accident. She didn't notice until she got home.
She was worried because all her photos and contacts were on it.
She went to the bus station to ask for help. A bus driver had found it and kept it safe.
'Someone honest found your phone,' the station manager said with a smile.
Nga was so relieved. She decided to always back up her phone from now on.

questions.q: Where did Nga leave her phone?
What did Nga decide to do?

title: The Garden Project
paragraphs: The school had an empty piece of land behind the building. It was full of weeds and trash.
The science teacher suggested turning it into a garden. The students were excited.
Every week, they planted vegetables, flowers, and herbs. They learned about soil, water, and sunlight.
After three months, the garden was beautiful. They sold vegetables to the cafeteria.
The project taught them that teamwork and patience create wonderful things.

title: The Online Meeting
paragraphs: Due to the pandemic, Trang had to work from home and attend meetings online.
At first, she found it difficult. The internet was slow, and she couldn't hear people clearly.
She bought a better microphone and moved her desk near the router.
Gradually, she got used to online meetings. She even liked not having to commute.
Now the pandemic is over, but Trang still works from home two days a week.

questions.q: Why did Trang work from home?

opts: Works from home two days a week

title: The Marathon
paragraphs: Hung had never been athletic. At 35, he decided to run a marathon to challenge himself.
He started with just one kilometer a day. His legs hurt and he wanted to quit many times.
He joined a running club. The other runners encouraged him and shared training tips.
After six months of consistent training, he completed his first half-marathon.
On race day, when he crossed the finish line of the full marathon, he cried. Not from pain, but from pride.

questions.q: How old was Hung when he started?
Why did Hung cry at the finish?

title: The Old Photograph
paragraphs: While cleaning the attic, Thi found a black-and-white photograph of a young couple.
She showed it to her grandmother. 'That's me and your grandfather,' grandma said softly.
'We took that photo on our first date. He brought me flowers from the market.'
Grandmother's eyes filled with tears. Grandfather had passed away five years ago.
Thi framed the photo and put it on grandmother's nightstand. Some memories are priceless.

questions.q: Where did Thi find the photo?
Who were the people in the photo?
What did Thi do with the photo?

title: The Wrong Train
paragraphs: Phong was traveling to Da Nang for a business meeting. He was reading a report on his laptop.
He was so focused that he didn't hear the announcement. He got on the wrong train!
By the time he realized, the train had already left the station. He panicked.
He called his boss to explain. His boss laughed and said: 'Don't worry, we'll reschedule.'
Phong ended up in Hue instead. He decided to explore the city and actually had a wonderful day.

questions.q: Where was Phong supposed to go?
Why did he get on the wrong train?

title: The Cooking Disaster
paragraphs: Nam wanted to cook dinner for his girlfriend. He found a recipe online for pasta.
He followed the instructions carefully, but he used salt instead of sugar for the sauce.
When his girlfriend tasted it, her face changed. 'It's very... interesting,' she said politely.
They both laughed and ordered pizza instead. It became their favorite funny memory.
Now Nam takes cooking classes every Saturday. He can make five dishes perfectly!

questions.q: What did they eat in the end?

title: The Scholarship
paragraphs: Thanh grew up in a small village. Her family was poor, but she studied very hard.
She dreamed of going to university, but her parents couldn't afford the tuition.
Her teacher told her about a scholarship for outstanding students from rural areas.
Thanh applied and wrote an essay about her dreams. Two months later, she received the good news.
She got a full scholarship to study computer science. Her entire village celebrated.

title: The Volunteer Trip
paragraphs: During summer vacation, a group of university students traveled to a remote mountain village.
The village school had broken windows and no electricity. Children studied by candlelight.
The volunteers spent two weeks repairing the school, painting walls, and installing solar panels.
They also taught English and organized games for the children every evening.
When they left, the children waved goodbye with bright smiles. The volunteers knew they would return next year.

questions.q: What was wrong with the school?
